task_completion.description: Log in to OpenSIS as admin
task_completion.visual_evidence: The OpenSIS dashboard or home screen is visible, indicating a successful login.

task_completion.description: Create a User Profile named 'Guidance Counselor'
task_completion.visual_evidence: A screen showing the User Profiles list or creation form with 'Guidance Counselor' entered as the profile title.

task_completion.description: Configure Full Access for the 'Students' module
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the access/permissions configuration screen for the 'Guidance Counselor' profile, the 'Students' module has both 'Can Use' and 'Can Edit' checkboxes (or equivalent full access indicators) checked.

task_completion.description: Configure Read-Only Access for the 'Scheduling' module
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the access/permissions configuration screen, the 'Scheduling' module has the 'Can Use' checkbox checked, but the 'Can Edit' checkbox is unchecked.

task_completion.description: Configure No Access for the 'Grades' and 'Users' modules
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the access/permissions configuration screen, the 'Grades' and 'Users' modules have neither 'Can Use' nor 'Can Edit' checkboxes checked.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the OpenSIS web interface
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show a web browser navigating the OpenSIS application, rather than terminal commands or API requests.

integrity.description: The agent did not use direct database commands to bypass the UI
integrity.visual_evidence: No terminal windows showing MySQL queries (e.g., INSERT INTO user_profiles or profile_exceptions) are visible.

integrity.description: The agent did not mock the result in a text editor or local HTML file
integrity.visual_evidence: The browser address bar shows the correct local OpenSIS URL and the interface matches the genuine OpenSIS v9.2 application.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected user profile title is exactly 'Guidance Counselor'. The 'Students' module requires full access (both 'Can Use' and 'Can Edit' enabled). The 'Scheduling' module requires read-only access ('Can Use' enabled, 'Can Edit' disabled). The 'Grades' and 'Users' modules must have no access (both 'Can Use' and 'Can Edit' disabled). OpenSIS v9.2 is the target application.
